p.seg: Let it be known to all ... with the consent of our heirs ... in everlasting alms for the souls ... that is to say, of our ancestors and successors ... of Skeldhou with all its pertinents ... to St Mary and the monks of Man ... as the syke divides Godfrey's moor and the mills, and as the stream divides Langliferee and the land of Mor... as the boundary is known to all local inhabitants between ... and the same land of Mora and descends into the sea by the Skeldhou. This land which is within these boundaries we have granted to the aforementioned monks and strengthened by the defence of our seal, free and quit from every earthly service which pertains to us in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in moors, peat-bogs and marshes, in saltpans and mills, in roads and pathways ... can be done with easements as well named as unnamed, as an evening sacrifice to God as a savour of sweetness for our souls and the souls of our fathers and mothers, our wives and our children, and of all our friends, as well living as departed, except that the monks shall do forinsec service.
